results.content: # Deep work sounds great…but who actually does it?” : r/productivity. Skip to main contentDeep work sounds great…but who actually does it?” : r/productivity. Image 1 Go to productivity. # Deep work sounds great…but who actually does it?”. How do you ACTUALLY create time for deep work in a world that doesn’t let you focus? This is also part of my life - deep work isn’t just about finding focus, it’s about doing something worth focusing on. Do you think this is fixable, or just the nature of corporate work? Who cares if you do all the tasks on time? I get at least 2 hours of deep work done everyday when working from home, if not more depending on that day's schedule. It's possible to do deep work at a job that doesn't necessarily accommodate this approach, but you'd only be able to do it in your own time and would end up working 10+ hours a day everyday.

results.content: # The Complete Guide to Deep Work. In fact, these activities won't aid you in any number of the ambitious goals you’ve set for yourself. To be truly exceptional at the work you do and to gain recognition for it, you’ll need to adopt a different strategy entirely. On the contrary, if you aren’t intentional about how you spend your time, your work hours slip away towards activities that Newport refers to as “shallow work”:. You’ll learn how to integrate deep work into your life in order to execute at a higher level and discover the rewards that come with regularly losing yourself in meaningful work. While you may be convinced of the value of deep work, you may be unsure of how to implement it in your life. * **The Monastic Philosophy of Deep Work Scheduling** is the most dedicated form of deep work and involves spending all of your working hours on a singular high-level focus.

results.title: Deep work: A complete guide - Timely
results.content: Most of us work in a state of perpetual distraction, multitasking and checking phones and emails every few minutes. Constant communication and immersive tech have made it almost impossible for us to stay focused on the complex, problem-solving tasks that push our work forward – ushering in huge consequences for our wellbeing, as well as our productivity. This also contributes to a culture of busyness, where we seem to work flat out, but with very little to show for it at the end of the day. We’ll then move on to lay out exactly how you can practice deep work and thrive by it. ## What is deep work? Deep work is the ability to concentrate deeply on a difficult task for prolonged periods of time without getting distracted. It creates that intense, out-of-body kind of focus that makes you completely oblivious to what’s going on around you – the kind that produces your best work.
